items.headline: U.S. Markets Close Mixed Amid Tech Rout and Sector Rotation
items.summary: U.S. stock markets concluded June 27, 2026, with a mixed performance across major indices. While AI-related stocks continued their upward momentum, a broader tech sell-off throughout the week, coupled with persistent inflation concerns and a hawkish Fed outlook, led to a defensive rotation. Healthcare, Real Estate, and Utilities sectors showed strength, while megacap tech names faced pressure. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Down 0.05% today. The index declined over 1% for the week.
Down 0.24% today. The tech-heavy Nasdaq dropped 4.6% on the week, marking its worst weekly loss in over a year.
Down 0.09% today. The Dow posted a weekly gain of 0.6% to 1%.

items.headline: ServiceNow Surges on AI Optimism
items.summary: AI-related stocks saw broad gains, with ServiceNow leading the charge.

items.headline: Snowflake Continues AI-Driven Ascent
items.summary: Another AI beneficiary, Snowflake, recorded strong daily gains.

items.headline: Workday Posts Significant Gains
items.summary: Workday's stock climbed as the AI sector outperformed.

items.headline: ON Semiconductor Plunges on Acquisition News
items.summary: ON Semiconductor experienced a sharp decline after announcing an agreement to acquire Synaptics in an all-stock deal.

items.headline: IREN Leads Weekly Crypto Stock Declines
items.summary: Crypto stock IREN retreated significantly over the past week amidst a broader Bitcoin sell-off.

items.headline: Nvidia Shares Tumble Amid Tech Sell-Off
items.summary: The AI chip maker, Nvidia, experienced a notable drop this week as investors rotated out of top tech stocks.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Stress Test Results Confirm Bank Resilience
items.summary: The Federal Reserve released its annual stress test results, indicating that large banks are resilient enough to withstand a severe economic downturn while continuing to support the economy. All 32 banks tested remained above minimum capital requirements.

items.headline: Key Inflation Gauge Reaches Three-Year High, Raising Rate Hike Expectations
items.summary: The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge rose to a new three-year high in May, with consumer prices increasing 4.1% year-over-year. Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari suggested a rate hike this year, attributing the pressure to the AI boom and persistent inflation.

items.headline: Micron Technology Reports Explosive Earnings Driven by AI Demand
items.summary: Micron Technology ($MU) announced strong Q3 fiscal 2026 earnings, reporting revenue of $41 billion, significantly surpassing expectations. The company's gross margin exceeded 84%, highlighting the robust demand for memory and storage solutions critical for the artificial intelligence revolution.

items.headline: Investors Rotate Out of Megacap Tech; Healthcare and Real Estate Lead
items.summary: The week saw a significant rotation out of top-performing technology stocks, particularly megacap and AI-driven names, into more defensive sectors. Healthcare gained 7.9% for the week, with Real Estate and Utilities also showing strong performance, as investors sought bargains outside of the pressured tech sector.

items.headline: Chinese Advisers Urge Fix for 'Two-Speed Economy' Amid AI Boom
items.summary: Advisers to the Chinese government called for measures to address a growing divergence in the economy, where the AI-fueled high-tech sector is outperforming while domestic consumer spending lags. Concerns were raised about the impact of this imbalance on technology innovation.
